# ADR-260006: Verification and acceptance gate model for public releases

Status: Accepted
Date: 2026-05-21
Owners: Repository owner

## Context

Section 8 needs explicit release-acceptance gates so deployment success is not treated as sufficient
quality evidence. The public site requires route/link integrity, app-sign-in target correctness,
and cross-device confidence before each production release is accepted.

## Decision

- Define a minimum mandatory release gate set:
  - landing page renders on deployed base path,
  - sign-in control is visible, clear, and points to app-domain sign-in,
  - Google Add-on, Contact, YouTube, Terms, Privacy, and Support links resolve correctly,
  - static-only behavior is preserved (no runtime backend dependency for public pages),
  - desktop and mobile smoke checks pass.
- Validation environments:
  - local preview (`npm run build` + `npm run preview`),
  - deployed GitHub Pages URL.
- Pass/fail discipline:
  - release is blocked if any mandatory gate fails.
- Sign-off authority:
  - repository owner performs final release acceptance.
- Regression handling:
  - post-release gate failure triggers revert-and-redeploy.
- Evidence policy:
  - each release keeps a short verification note with URL, timestamp, reviewer, and gate outcomes.

## Alternatives Considered

- CI-build-only acceptance: Rejected because it misses routing/link and UX regressions.
- Manual subjective review only: Rejected because pass/fail criteria become inconsistent.
- Full automated browser suite as initial hard gate: Rejected for now due to setup overhead relative
  to current project scope.

## Consequences

- Positive: Releases become auditable and repeatable.
- Positive: Faster triage when regressions happen.
- Negative: Slightly longer release checklist execution time.
- Risk: Gate drift can occur if criteria are not kept aligned with route/content changes.

## Verification

- Mandatory checks are listed in release verification notes.
- All checks are performed on local preview and deployed Pages URL.
- Sign-off identity and timestamp are recorded.

## Rollback

If any accepted release is later found to violate mandatory gates, revert the offending commit,
redeploy, and reopen verification before re-release.
